Title: llvm-primer/phi.md at master · prosyslab-classroom/llvm-primer · GitHub
URL: https://github.com/prosyslab-classroom/llvm-primer/blob/master/phi.md
Content: ## Navigation Menu. # Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests... # Provide feedback. We read every piece of feedback, and take your input very seriously. # Saved searches. ## Use saved searches to filter your results more quickly. ## FilesExpand file tree. ## Footer. ## File metadata and controls. In SSA form, a variable is assigned and. updated at exactly one code point. If a variable in the source code has multiple assignments,. these assignments are split into separate variables in the LLVM IR and then merged back together. We call this merge point a **phi node**. To illustrate phi nodes, consider the following code:. Depending on the value of `y`, we either take the left branch and execute `x++`, or the right branch and execute `x--`. assign `%inc` to `%x` if the then branch is taken, or `%dec` to `%x` if the else branch was taken. Therefore, you should be very careful when you change LLVM IR code. ### Footer navigation.

Title: What exactly PHI instruction does and how to use it in LLVM
URL: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11485531/what-exactly-phi-instruction-does-and-how-to-use-it-in-llvm
Content: ### your communities. ### more stack exchange communities. Communities for your favorite technologies. Stack Overflow for Teams is now called **Stack Internal**. # What exactly PHI instruction does and how to use it in LLVM. *The 'phi' instruction is used to implement the φ node in the SSA graph representing the function.*. Could someone explain what is a Phi node, and how to implement `||` with it? A phi node is an instruction used to select a value depending on the predecessor of the current block (Look here to see the full hierarchy - it's also used as a value, which is one of the classes which it inherits from). Phi nodes are necessary due to the structure of the SSA (static single assignment) style of the LLVM code - for example, the following C++ function. %r.addr = alloca i8, align 1. Otherwise, it is equal to the value of `%tobool2` - and that's exactly what we get from the following IR line:.

Title: LLVM Tutorial #17: The Φ (Phi) Function
URL: https://tobyho.com/video/LLVM-Tutorial-17-The-F-(Phi)-Function.html
Content: # LLVM Tutorial #17: The Φ (Phi) Function. In this series I walkthrough the LLVM "Kaleidoscope" Tutorial, where you follow step by step to create your first programming language frontend using LLVM as the backend. Last time we wrote the lexer and the parser for the if statement. This time, we talk more about SSA, and more specifically, about the Φ (Phi) function. * 00:00) : hey it's Toby welcome back to the LLVM. * 00:02) : tutorial walkthrough we're in the middle. * 00:10) : for for the if statement or I should say. * 00:15) : the if expression we weren't able to. * 00:21) : we're supposed to do that but before. * 00:23) : doing that I took a detour because the. * 00:30) : to follow it it's telling us that if we. * 00:35) : write an if statement like this in the. * 01:23) : if you copy this code and and put it.
